error_signature: new row created instead of updating existing row
title: New row created instead of updating existing row in Google Sheets
symptom: When attempting to update a row in Google Sheets using a unique key, a new row is created instead of overwriting the existing one.
applies_when: Unique key is intended to overwrite existing rows
not_this_if: Unique key is not formatted consistently between input and sheet
root_cause: The issue arises because the unique key is being treated as text in the input but as a number in the Google Sheets column. This mismatch leads to the system failing to recognize that the keys are the same, resulting in the creation of a new row instead of updating the existing one.
fix_steps: Change the formatting of the relevant column in Google Sheets to plain text.
Ensure that there are no whitespaces before '{{' or after '}}' in your expressions.
Test the workflow again with the same unique key.
agent_instruction: Change the Google Sheets column format to plain text and check for whitespaces in expressions.
